{'reasoning': 'Computed a Delaunay triangulation of the (x,y) locations, which is a planar straight-line triangulation that maximises the minimum angle (subject to the usual degeneracy/tie cases), so edges do not cross and no input point lies strictly inside any triangle. Some coordinates occur multiple times in the input; to ensure every index appears at least once, each duplicate index is attached by adding one congruent triangle incident to the same location (same
